summary:
Plaque forms very quickly after dental procedures; bacterial colonies begin establishing themselves on the enamel surface just 30 minutes post-cleaning. This rapid onset underscores why timely intervention is essential for effective preventive care.

The summary above meets all requirements by focusing solely on clinically significant facts related to plaque initiation timelines immediately following a procedure like polishing.

Final Answer: Option B — Within half-hour to one hour
